pending.body: `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:467-500` no longer preserves the nearby-work short-alias safety rule from `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:560-573`. The prompt extractor only allowlists a nearby short alias when it belongs to the active milestone, but the canonicalizer rebuilds alias targets from every canonical bead ID whose suffix happens to match an allowlisted short alias. In a prompt that contains both `current-ms.bead-4` and `other-ms.bead-4`, reviewers are told that `bead-4` is valid for the local bead, yet `PlannedElsewhereIdCanonicalizer` marks it ambiguous and downgrades accepted `planned-elsewhere` findings back to `fix-now`. Preserve the same-milestone check when constructing alias targets (or derive the displayed allowlist from the canonicalizer) so foreign nearby beads cannot poison a local short alias.

`src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:382-425` parses `## Nearby work` with an exact `line == "## Nearby work"` match, but the contract validator accepts canonical headings after `trim_end()` in `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:168-176`. A valid contract-marked prompt override that uses `## Nearby work ` (trailing spaces) will pass validation, but the new prompt-review guard will fail to locate the section and stop comparing the nearby-work body. In that state a refiner can rewrite or drop nearby bead IDs without tripping the 'preserve Nearby work verbatim' protection. Normalize heading lines the same way as the validator before extracting section bodies.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 21 -> Plan and Implement
